candidates.context: Entertainment-trade outlet with editorial standards and named authors; used to independently confirm the Muse Image feature removal (meta-muse-image-consent update, cycle 3, 2026-07-11) alongside Deadline. Propose Tier 2, type html or rss (check for a feed).

Entertainment-trade outlet with editorial standards and named authors; used to independently confirm the Muse Image feature removal (meta-muse-image-consent update, cycle 3, 2026-07-11) alongside TheWrap. Also referenced (unlogged) in the original meta-muse-image-consent verify pass on 2026-07-09 for CAA/SAG-AFTRA reaction corroboration. Propose Tier 2, type html or rss (check for a feed).

Used as an unregistered Tier-2-equivalent source (per verification.md §1) to corroborate Grok 4.5 pricing/spec claims in grok-4-5-release. Established tech-industry outlet with editorial standards and named authors; propose for registration as Tier 2, type rss (feed: https://www.infoworld.com/index.rss, unverified this run).

Independent third-party AI model evaluator (nonprofit, publishes its own capability/safety evaluations). Directly load-bearing for the GPT-5.6 reward-hacking finding in gpt-5-6-launch -- this is exactly the kind of independent-reproduction source the capability-claim standard (verification.md §3) calls for. Strong candidate for Tier 1 registration; likely to recur for future frontier-model launches.

Independent third-party AI benchmark/leaderboard org, confirmed real via direct fetch (not hallucinated). Used as the only genuinely independent capability evidence in meta-muse-spark-1-1. IMPORTANT: the leaderboard updates continuously -- a first-pass verifier cited stale/wrong figures from memory/search-snippet reconstruction, and only a direct live re-fetch during adversarial review caught the discrepancy. Recommend registering as Tier 2, but any future citation must re-fetch live rather than recall previous figures.

Meta's own developer blog, same publisher as the already-registered ai.meta.com (Meta AI Blog), used for pricing/API details the main blog post omitted. Recommend adding as a second Tier-0 URL under Meta's entry rather than a new source, since it's the same institutional origin.

Meta/Instagram's own help-center documentation, directly load-bearing for the Muse Image opt-out mechanism claim. Tier 0, same corporate origin as Meta AI Blog but a distinct product-documentation domain -- worth a registry note since it may recur for Meta product-behavior stories.

Legal-specialty press with detailed, accurate court-filing coverage (case number, docket details) for the NYT v. OpenAI sanctions motion. Likely to be useful again for AI litigation stories, which are recurring (copyright suits, safety liability). Propose Tier 1-2 candidate.

Entertainment/media trade press; useful for talent-industry and media-litigation reactions to AI stories (CAA/SAG-AFTRA on Meta Muse Image; NYT copyright case detail). Direct fetch is frequently paywalled (HTTP 402 via a tollbit.variety.com redirect observed this run) -- note this as a known access limitation if registered.
Independent quality press with strong EU policy/regulatory coverage including named MEP quotes and reaction framing. Useful for EU AI Act / regulatory stories going forward. Propose Tier 2.

Specialist legal/regulatory press, paywalled but precise on regulatory and litigation framing; corroborated both the EU cybersecurity plan's non-binding framing and the NYT/OpenAI litigation. Propose Tier 2 candidate for regulatory + AI-litigation stories.

Major business outlet, used to corroborate Muse Spark pricing (confirmed) and the Meta AI chip production report (did not add independence -- also cites the same Reuters memo). Propose Tier 2 for business/pricing corroboration.
Apple/tech-focused outlet; independently corroborated the OpenAI Atlas shutdown (sourced from the same James Sun X post as Android Authority, but written independently). Propose Tier 2.
Used twice this run for independent corroboration (OpenAI Atlas shutdown, Anthropic Claude Reflect). Propose Tier 2.
R&D World; long-running tech/science trade outlet, confirmed legitimate. Carried the METR reward-hacking finding accurately. Propose Tier 2.

Independent AI benchmark tracker (distinct from vals.ai). Used for partial corroboration of GPT-5.6 coding benchmark claims. Propose Tier 2 alongside vals.ai as an independent-benchmark source, with the same live-recheck caveat.

English-language South Korean daily with named reporters and company-disclosure-level financial detail (SK Hynix IPO use-of-proceeds breakdown). Used as an independent Tier-2 source for sk-hynix-nasdaq-ipo. Propose Tier 2, type html.

English edition of Seoul Economic Daily, a Korean financial-press outlet with distinct investment-schedule detail (Yongin/Cheongju fab spend by year) not just a wire re-print. Used alongside Korea Herald as a second independent source for sk-hynix-nasdaq-ipo proceeds claims. Propose Tier 2, type html.

Established tech-hardware trade outlet with named authors and original semiconductor-industry framing (not a wire re-print). Used for sk-hynix-nasdaq-ipo. Propose Tier 2, type rss (feed likely available, unverified this run).

Live financial-data source (not editorial), used for a direct timestamped live-fetch of first-day trading data per the live-fetch rule for third-party numbers (sk-hynix-nasdaq-ipo). Propose as a data-source reference rather than a Tier 2 editorial source -- useful for future stock/market claims needing a live check.

Xinhua (PRC state news agency) English site. Used as Tier-0-equivalent primary source for Xi Jinping's WAIC 2026 full-text keynote and the WAICO founding announcement (china-waico-ai-cooperation-body) -- appropriate for reporting the verbatim content of an official PRC statement/speech, but note it is state media, not independent-confirmation-eligible for business/business-adjacent claims. Propose Tier 0 (official-statement-only use), flagged as state-affiliated.

CGTN (PRC state broadcaster) English site, used alongside Xinhua as a second full-text transcript source for the same WAIC 2026 keynote. Same state-affiliation caveat as english.news.cn -- propose Tier 0 for official-statement transcripts only.

PRC State Council Information Office. Republishes official government/speech content. Used as a government-primary corroboration in china-waico-ai-cooperation-body. Propose Tier 0, official-statement-only, state-affiliated.

Established enterprise-tech outlet with editorial standards and named authors; provided independent (non-Chinese-state) confirmation of WAICO membership composition and the absence of the US/EU/UK/Japan/South Korea in china-waico-ai-cooperation-body. Propose Tier 2.
Established enterprise-tech outlet; independent confirmation of WAICO country count and Western-absence framing in china-waico-ai-cooperation-body. Propose Tier 2.

AI-focused newsletter/alert site used as independent Tier<=2 corroboration for the AISI cheating-behaviour story (aisi-cheating-behaviour-evaluations, cycle 1, 2026-07-21). Editorial standards/named authorship not yet assessed -- propose for review before registration.

flagged_not_recommended.context: Claimed GPT-5.6 GA was restricted to 'vetted organizations at the US government's request' -- uncorroborated by any other outlet and contradicted by the primary OpenAI source and TechCrunch. Excluded from published claims. Do not register without independent corroboration of its other reporting.

Used only to reconstruct a paywalled Variety story's quotes on the Meta Muse Image CAA/SAG-AFTRA reaction; appears to relay Variety rather than report independently. Not a genuine independent source -- do not register.
Surfaced corroborating the OpenAI Atlas shutdown, but reads as an AI-news aggregator rather than a staffed newsroom. Needs editorial-standards vetting before any registration consideration.

query_notes: 'Grok 4.5 xAI release SWE-bench benchmark' and site:x.ai searches both returned high-signal, on-topic results the same day as the release — worth keeping as a discovery.yaml pattern for major-model-release verification (site-scoped search as x.ai/news fetch fallback, per the known-blocker note in the run brief).

For frontier-model launches, a search pattern like '{model_name} independent benchmark review' or '{model_name} METR OR vals.ai OR artificialanalysis.ai' reliably surfaced independent, non-vendor evaluators (METR for GPT-5.6, Vals AI + Artificial Analysis for GPT-5.6/Muse Spark) — worth promoting to a recurring claim_check_template alongside the existing '{model_name} independent benchmark' line, since these three named evaluators recurred across multiple stories this run.

'{company} response OR denies OR disputes {allegation}' was essential for the NYT/OpenAI litigation story to surface OpenAI's on-record denial alongside the plaintiffs' claims -- worth adding as an explicit claim_check_template for any business/legal claim type, not just capability claims.

For business claims sourced to a single wire/leak (Reuters exclusive on Meta's AI chip), tracing every outlet's citation back to one origin was decisive in correctly failing that story's gate -- a reminder that broad press pickup of a single leak is not multi-source confirmation.

Tier-0 html sweep (Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, Ai2, NIST, EU Commission) via WebFetch worked well this run and surfaced 3 of the 7 published stories directly (Anthropic Reflect, Meta Muse Image, Meta Muse Spark, EU cybersecurity plan) that would otherwise have relied only on secondary press framing -- confirms this sweep is worth the run-time cost every night, not just when a Tier-2 story needs it.

2026-07-10 night cycle 1: for a court-filing story (Apple v. OpenAI), 'site:courtlistener.com {case caption}' reliably located the actual docket (case number, court, parties) as a primary-ish anchor even when the full docket page itself 403'd on direct fetch -- search-index metadata was still usable to confirm case identity. Worth adding as a claim_check_template for litigation claims: '{plaintiff} v. {defendant} site:courtlistener.com'.

candidates_2026-07-10_night_c1.context: Free public court-record aggregator (RECAP/PACER mirror) -- used as the primary-document anchor for the Apple v. OpenAI trade-secrets filing (case number, court, parties). Direct full-page fetch 403'd for both verifier and falsifier agents, but the docket page's search-index metadata was independently usable to confirm case identity. Strong candidate for Tier 0/1 registration given how often AI litigation is recurring in coverage (this is the second litigation story this week after nyt-openai-copyright-evidence-dispute).

Apple-focused trade press with named authors; carried the most granular detail (metal-finishing technique, vendor-sourcing allegations) not found elsewhere in the Apple v. OpenAI coverage. Propose Tier 2.
Business/fintech-adjacent trade press; directly quoted Fidji Simo's own post and Altman's reply, fetched cleanly when CNBC/Axios/Bloomberg were blocked. Propose Tier 2.
Markets/business news outlet; independently fetchable, quoted Simo's post directly and verbatim -- useful when other outlets are paywalled/blocked. Propose Tier 2.
Apple-focused trade press, established editorial standards. Corroborated the Apple v. OpenAI filing. Propose Tier 2.

Major independent news outlet; corroborated the February 2026 warning-letter detail in the Apple v. OpenAI story via what reads as separate AP-wire-sourced reporting, not just a relay. Propose Tier 2.

candidates_2026-07-12_night_c1.context: Major wire/business outlet; appeared independently reporting the Bernanke/Anthropic LTBT appointment (anthropic-bernanke-ltbt) alongside CNBC. Not fetched directly this cycle (paywall/video-style page) so not used as a listed source, but recurring enough across nights to warrant Tier 2 registration consideration.

candidates_2026-07-12_night_c2.context: AI-product-tracking trade outlet; independently corroborated the Robostral Navigate description (mistral-robostral-navigate) alongside Bloomberg. Recurred already this run cycle as a same-day model-release tracker; worth watching for further recurrence before proposing Tier 2.

candidates_2026-07-12_night_c3.context: South China Morning Post; major independent English-language outlet with named byline, used to independently confirm the EdgeBench/scaling-law story (bytedance-edgebench-scaling-law) is a genuine, real report and not fabricated -- did not independently verify the underlying figures, just relayed ByteDance's own numbers. Propose Tier 2, good general China-tech-industry coverage source.

candidates_2026-07-13_night2_c2.context: Established tech outlet; used in the openai-safety-heidecke-departure verify pass, but note it (like every other outlet checked) relays Wired's exclusive rather than reporting independently -- does not itself add source independence. Propose Tier 2 for future use where it does have its own reporting.
Established tech outlet; same caveat as engadget.com in this run -- relays Wired's exclusive on the Heidecke departure, not independent reporting. Propose Tier 2 for future use.

Tech news outlet; relays Wired's exclusive on the Heidecke/OpenAI safety reorg (openai-safety-heidecke-departure), same independence caveat. Propose Tier 2 candidate, evaluate against its other reporting first.

candidates_2026-07-16_night_c1.context: OpenAI's official system-card/deployment-safety hub -- Tier-0 primary, distinct domain from the registered 'OpenAI News' feed (openai.com/news). Load-bearing for gpt-5-6-sol-file-deletion (severity-level-3 misalignment classification, VM 1-3/5-7 deletion test case). Direct PDF fetch returned corrupted binary this cycle; used via independent secondary quotation instead. Likely to recur for every future OpenAI model launch -- propose Tier 0 registration, type html (hub page) or a per-model PDF pattern.

candidates_2026-07-13_night2_c4.context: Independent San Francisco local news outlet with named bylines; primary independent source for the sf-stop-the-ai-race-protest story, own reporting/photos not derived from wire. Propose Tier 2.
Independent San Francisco local nonprofit newsroom; independently corroborated sf-stop-the-ai-race-protest alongside SF Standard and AP. Propose Tier 2.

UC Berkeley student paper; used only as the (single-outlet) source of the 'largest AI protest in American history' characterization, explicitly not treated as independent confirmation of that superlative. Lower priority for registration than the other two this cycle.

candidates_2026-07-16_night_c4.context: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab -- own blog + model-card hub, Tier-0 primary for the Inkling model release (975B/41B MoE, Apache 2.0). Load-bearing for thinking-machines-inkling-release. First model release from this lab; likely to recur. Propose Tier 0 registration, type html.

candidates_2026-07-17_night_c3.context: Official NY State governor's office -- Tier-0 primary, load-bearing for ny-hochul-data-center-moratorium (full Executive Order 62 text). Likely to recur for future NY state AI/tech policy actions. Propose Tier 0 registration, type html.
NY public radio/news network; independently corroborated Hochul's public response to Trump's criticism for ny-hochul-data-center-moratorium. Propose Tier 2.

National policy/politics outlet; carried the Data Center Coalition's on-record industry-reaction statement, added for balance per the falsifier's instruction on ny-hochul-data-center-moratorium. Propose Tier 2.

candidates_2026-07-17_night_c4.context: Canadian tech-industry trade press with named authors and its own reporting; independently confirmed the full eight-institution list and figures for anthropic-canada-ai-research-funding, not merely relaying Anthropic's page. Likely to recur for Canadian AI-industry stories. Propose Tier 2.

University of Toronto's own institutional newsroom -- confirmed its Data Sciences Institute's $1M portion of the Anthropic Canada funding in its own voice. Treated as a one-off institutional primary for anthropic-canada-ai-research-funding rather than a recurring scan source; not proposing for the general registry, noting here for the record.

candidates_2026-07-18_night_c5.context: Established tech-news outlet, independently bylined; used as a corroborating source for xai-spacexai-rebrand. Likely to recur. Propose Tier 2.
AI-industry news site, independently bylined; used as a corroborating source for xai-spacexai-rebrand. Moderate recurrence in AI-news searches this week -- propose Tier 2, revisit if quality dips.

candidates_2026-07-19_night_c4.context: Second recurrence (see also base 'candidates' list, first seen 2026-07-10 on Apple v. OpenAI). Direct fetch of axios.com returned HTTP 403 for both verifier and falsifier this cycle; both used a reader-proxy to get the actual article text rather than relying on search snippets. Original source for the PAC's Q2 FEC cash-on-hand figure in leading-the-future-pac-31m-midterms, independently corroborated against Transformer News and CNBC. Note: this outlet issued a correction on the cited article ($31.5M -> $31M) after initial publication -- worth flagging that Axios pieces on fast-moving filings may need a re-check for corrections. Recurrence across two separate nights strengthens the case for Tier 2 registration.

outlets_checked_but_not_used_this_run.note: Andreessen/Horowitz/Brockman donor-amount figures were found via two OTHER Axios pieces (30 Jan and 16 Apr 2026) but excluded from the published article as stale/conflated with the Q2 disclosure -- see data/verified/leading-the-future-pac-31m-midterms.json notes.

candidates_2026-07-19_night_c4.context: Independent FEC-filing tracker specifically for AI-industry-linked political committees (pulls directly from FEC.gov, not editorial commentary). Used to independently corroborate Leading the Future's $31M cash-on-hand figure for leading-the-future-pac-31m-midterms without relying on Axios's framing of the same underlying filing. Likely to recur for future AI-industry PAC/election-spending stories this cycle (Public First, Jobs and Democracy PAC, Think Big PAC, American Mission PAC are all trackable via the same tool). Propose as a data-source reference (like stockanalysis.com), useful for the live-fetch rule on any future PAC financial claim.

candidates_2026-07-19_night_c3.context: Independent individual security researcher; published a reproducible wire-level analysis (mitmproxy capture, canary-file proof) of grok-build's data-upload behavior -- load-bearing primary technical evidence for xai-grok-build-privacy-incident, directly fetched and confirmed by both verifier and falsifier passes. Not a staffed newsroom, so not a typical Tier-2 candidate, but the technical rigor (reproducible proof, not just a claim) argues for a narrower policy note on when individual security-research writeups qualify as Tier 1-2 evidence rather than blanket Tier-3 discovery-only treatment. Flagging for source-lifecycle review rather than a straight registration proposal.

Third occurrence logged for techtimes.com (see also candidates_2026-07-13_night2_c2) -- this time reporting that the grok-build upload code path remains present in the newly open-sourced repo, merely disabled by a server-side flag. Found via search only this cycle, not independently re-fetched, so NOT used as evidence in xai-grok-build-privacy-incident; the claim was dropped as a watch item instead. Recurrence across three separate stories now strengthens the case for Tier 2 registration.

candidates_2026-07-19_night_c5.context: Used as the most authoritative direct syndicator of a paywalled Financial Times scoop (Apple preservation letters to ~40 ex-employees now at OpenAI) for an in-place update to apple-openai-trade-secrets-lawsuit -- both verifier and falsifier independently re-fetched. Established Apple-beat outlet, editorial standards, named authors; recurs across multiple Apple/OpenAI-lawsuit nights this month. Propose Tier 2.

Same story and rationale as macrumors.com above -- second direct syndicator of the FT scoop, independently re-fetched by both verifier and falsifier. Established Apple-beat outlet; also recurs across multiple Apple/OpenAI-lawsuit nights this month. Propose Tier 2.
